headline: Energy and Industrials lead as rotation compresses breadth gains
market_take: OBX outpaces OSEBX by 3.43 points, reflecting large-cap concentration in the Energy and Industrials clusters that are driving the rotation regime. Breadth sits at 0.64, outside the 50%±20% neutral band on the upside, indicating that participation is wider than the leadership names alone suggest. Stretched mean-reversion readings across multiple names signal that recent gains are clustered in an increasingly extended subset of the index.

drivers.claim: Top-decile momentum and alpha confirm $OKEA as clear Energy leadership.
$HSHP carries the highest risk-adjusted return among all drivers.
$SUBC posts strong YTD returns with quality-leader Sharpe across Energy.
$SMOP shows maximum momentum with six-month alpha stretched well above peers.

risks.claim: $KIT is overextended at near-double its mean-reversion threshold.
$KOG carries the deepest drawdown risk among overextended names.
$SUBC sits in both driver and risk buckets; extension warrants close monitoring.
$BWE volatility percentile flags crowded positioning at elevated levels.

watchlist.claim: Near-buy for $MPCC held back by mean_reversion gate not yet cleared.
$SNI near-buy candidate blocked by mean_reversion threshold remaining elevated.
